channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: China and Myanmar detain U.S. citizens in separate incidents, sparking alarm
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Tan
item.description: Despite no immediate evidence that they were connected, the detentions of two vocal commentators on Myanmar triggered alarm in the wider research community.

item.title: Defending migrants, Pope Leo calls for ‘legal’ paths, a nod to fraught politics
item.dc:creator: Anthony Faiola
item.description: As he finished a seven-day visit, Pope Leo articulated a fuller position on migration, including the “right” of nations to protect their borders, that his critics often ignore.

item.title: Why a notably warm start to the year foreshadows what’s ahead
item.dc:creator: Ben Noll
item.description: 2027 has notably high odds of becoming the warmest year on record, with the latest projections showing nearly an 80 percent chance.

item.title: Behind veneer of normalcy in Dubai, war fatigue and a sense of uncertainty
item.dc:creator: Rachel Chason
item.description: In the United Arab Emirates, there is a growing sense that the U.S.-Israeli war has taught Tehran how to menace its adversaries even without developing a nuclear bomb.

item.title: Trump says he has canceled plans to strike Iran, claiming deal is near
item.dc:creator: Victoria Craw, Siham Shamalakh, Dan Diamond
item.description: President Donald Trump withdrew threats to take Iran’s most critical oil terminal “in the not too distant future,” after fresh attacks on U.S. bases in the region.

item.title: El Niño is officially here, bringing domino weather effects across the planet
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cappucci, Ben Noll
item.description: By the time an El Niño is declared, it means Earth’s atmosphere is already beginning to respond.

item.title: U.S. launches new strikes on Iran, hours after Trump issued warning
item.dc:creator: Victoria Craw, Michael Birnbaum, Heba Farouk Mahfouz , Joyce Lee, Meg Kelly
item.description: U.S. Central Command said it went after “multiple targets” after the president said Tehran would “pay the price” for not making a deal.

item.title: A new wave of anti-immigrant violence hits U.K. as riots convulse Belfast
item.dc:creator: Amanda Ferguson, Steve Hendrix
item.description: Attackers torched neighborhoods across Belfast, the capital of Northern Ireland, after a Sudanese asylum seeker was arrested and accused of stabbing a man.
